rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace and resolve the first invalid identifier on the same line.
generic_rules: Validator diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers should be addressed by correcting or removing the invalid AGREE clause.
AGREE contracts must reference only visible AADL features or helpers declared in the same annex.
Repair AGREE contracts by resolving root causes of reference errors rather than adding new architecture elements.
When a property association cannot be resolved, verify that the target is a valid AADL feature or property.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
AGREE guarantees must reference valid expressions with types; invalid expressions often stem from unresolved identifiers.
anti_patterns: Avoid adding new architecture elements to fix AGREE reference errors; instead, resolve or remove the invalid clause.
Do not assume that missing references can be resolved by adding new components or features without checking visibility.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations not pointing to anything
AGREE validator diagnostics about missing annex placement or assume/guarantee syntax
